global.title: Senate passes major ICE and border funding bill, House vote next
global.summary: The U.S. Senate approved a $70 billion bill to fund ICE and border patrol through the rest of President Trump's term. The measure now heads to the House after weeks of Republican infighting.
global.whyItMatters: Immigration pressures shape pastoral care, public prayer, and how churches think about mercy, law, and welcome.

global.title: Trump says he would meet Iran’s supreme leader if tied to peace plan
global.summary: President Trump said he would be open to meeting Iran’s supreme leader as part of a plan to end the war, even as U.S. and Iranian officials disagree over whether negotiations are making real progress.
global.whyItMatters: This is a key flashpoint for prayer, peacemaking language, and wise pastoral response to fear around war in the Middle East.

global.title: House passes resolution seeking to curb Trump’s war powers in Iran
global.summary: A House resolution aimed at limiting President Trump’s military authority in Iran has advanced, signaling growing concern in Washington over the scope of the conflict.
global.whyItMatters: Pastors often help congregations process war, national power, and conscience; this signals the conflict is becoming a deeper constitutional and moral issue.

canadian.title: Kevin O'Leary to shrink size of Utah data center project
canadian.summary: Canadian businessman Kevin O'Leary is scaling back a major Utah data center project, a sign of caution around large tech infrastructure investments.
canadian.whyItMatters: Canadian leaders and churches watching AI, energy use, and economic volatility may see this as another sign of a cooling, uncertain investment climate.

persecutedChurch.title: Nigeria sentences four to death over Owo Catholic church attack which killed 41
persecutedChurch.summary: A Nigerian court sentenced four men to death over the 2022 attack on St. Francis Catholic Church in Owo, where 41 worshippers were killed. The ruling follows an accelerated high-profile trial.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: This is a sobering reminder to pray for grieving believers in Nigeria and to keep the global church before the congregation.

persecutedChurch.title: Story says Pentagon dropped 180 faiths from recognized list
persecutedChurch.summary: A Military Religious Freedom Foundation report says the U.S. military has removed many minority faiths from its recognized religions list, including Wiccans, Humanists, and others.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: Religious liberty concerns are worth watching closely, especially where state power may narrow protections for minority beliefs and chaplaincy care.

leadership.title: Why Calm, Not Charisma, Builds Teams That Perform Under Pressure
leadership.summary: A Forbes leadership piece argues that trust, calm presence, radical transparency, and shared problem-solving matter more than forceful personality when teams face pressure.
leadership.whyItMatters: Helpful counsel for pastoral leadership: steady presence and honest communication often strengthen staff and volunteers more than intensity does.

goodNews.title: Oscar-winning actress Kate Winslet delights young film fan with surprise call
goodNews.summary: Kate Winslet surprised a blind teenage girl with a phone call after hearing she had to travel 140 miles to find an accessible cinema screening. The moment also renewed calls for better accessibility.
goodNews.whyItMatters: A gracious story of kindness and advocacy that could encourage a sermon or pastoral reflection on dignity, inclusion, and noticing those often overlooked.
